entries.notes: Bound root. Reduplicated (S3-S3) it nominalizes to 'human' — see that entry.
Reduplication of a property root is the productive rule 'a being characterized by X' — compare Eridian (S4-S4).
Bound root. Reduplicated (S4-S4) it nominalizes to 'Eridian' — see that entry.

entries.gloss: Eridian, a native of this world
entries.notes: Reduplication of a property root is the productive rule 'a being characterized by X' — compare human (S3-S3).
Adjacent scale degree to 'bad' (S6) — antonym pairs sit one degree apart.
Adjacent scale degree to 'good' (S5) — antonym pairs sit one degree apart.
Conventionally spoken at register +1 or +2 — see docs/eridian.md#register.
Distinct from the NEG particle, which negates a predicate rather than answering one.
Doubles as the sentence-final question particle — see docs/eridian.md#grammar.
Copula, used only for noun-to-noun identity statements — adjectives and verbs need no copula.
Tense suffix, attached directly after the verb as part of the same word — no other conjugation exists.
Tense suffix, attached directly after the verb as part of the same word — no other conjugation exists.
